steps.title: Identify the temporal signal
steps.content: The first sentence states that Salazar's 1873 discovery “went against earlier assumptions about the brain.” This setup signals that the next sentence explains what those earlier assumptions were—establishing a temporal relationship between past beliefs and a discovery that contradicted them.

steps.title: Match the transition to the sequence
steps.content: "Previously" directly marks the temporal shift—scientists held this belief before Salazar's discovery. The other three choices fail to establish the necessary temporal relationship between past assumptions and the new discovery. That is the correct answer.
